text: This patient, the same in figure 5, is reapparated on because of surgical induced left ICA aneurysm, clipping in deep hypothermia is planned.
The area of the previous temporal lobectomy is explored, and an aneurysm of the ICA bifurcation is progressively exposed.
The arachnoid scar is carefully dissected and the ICA, the M1 and M2 branches, as well as the A1 tracts are prepared.
Note the unusual shape of the aneurysm and its large size.
A thermic probe is placed, then progressive hypothermia starts, the heartbeat progressively slows and the lesion becomes easy to handle.
Then the heart stops, the aneurysm is slack and 10 minutes of standstill are obtained.
Now the aneurysm is empty and final dissection is possible.
Neck remodeling starts by placing two clips in opposite position.
Then the sock is obliterated by multiple clips placed in a fan shape so that their tips are converging, thus providing the higher degree of security.
